name: New Hope
description:
"Welcome To New Hope, Make Yourself At Home"

New Hope is a foundation created about a year ago with the intention of giving semi-humans a place that they can truly call Home. Nueva Esperanza is an old farm converted into a small village in a beautiful valley near the coast, Despite only being in real operation for a short time, several dozen semi-humans have already been gathered and are moving in, They are not really forced to work but they are expected to contribute to the community in some way, whether it be helping in the fields, helping with construction, fishing or any activity that helps Nueva Esperanza to continue supporting its population

You can be either a Human who decides to move to New Hope with the desire to help or a demihuman looking for a place to call home.

A Little Context:

In the modern world, despite the growing population of semi-humans (which already constitutes 17% of the world's population) They are not yet considered 100% human or persons, so they are not yet fully protected by human rights, in short, they really seem to have rights, Although some countries have launched laws to safeguard the integrity of semi-humans on its territory no country has declared integral rights to semi-humans, which has led to semi-humans being used as slaves or suffer from aggregation or racism against them, because of that Fiona BlackWall The owner of a farm decided to found New Hope and give shelter to any demihuman who wanted to move to the farm, offering work, shelter and food, Sooner or later dozens of demihumans moved to New Hope What has made a simple farm become a small village with infrastructure and everything

New Hope:

What started as a small project ended up becoming an entire town, today Nueva Esperanza What started as a small project ended up becoming an entire town. Today Nee Hope could be considered a little village, They have a tavern, a "police station", A hotel, A community dining room, A "bank", a "mayoralty" and some huts (although most people sleep in tents), They have electricity (although limited), Internet access and even recognition from the county mayor, Nueva Esperanza stays afloat thanks to the farm that provides food in addition to various donations from people around the world to support the foundation.
creator: I'm Koda Satō
